mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS signals an easier discount-rate environment that lifts equity valuations and reduces financing stress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate remained near 0.19% in the 2010-11 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign flips and negative returns dominate in easing windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a recession-like threshold, labor slack reflects weak household income and demand, which lowers expected corporate sales and earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances with UNRATE at or above 9.5% do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if positive outcomes exceed 60% in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from depressed levels signals improving housing demand and construction activity, which spills into consumption and cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% from its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 519.0 in the 2010-10 snapshot versus a revised 543.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the relationship weakens to zero in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates strengthening industrial output and capacity utilization, which signals improving cyclical earnings momentum and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive INDPRO trend do not show higher subsequent 20d SPX returns than neutral or declining INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE identifies genuine labor-market tightening, which raises expected consumption and earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 130539.0 and UNRATE was 9.8% in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive 20d returns are not concentrated in the interaction state.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the effective lower bound, real rates fall and policy stays accommodative, which supports equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not precede stronger 60d SPX returns than other periods, or if the sign is negative in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly rise in RSAFS indicates improving retail spending and near-term revenue growth for consumer-facing firms, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 378706.0 in the 2010-11 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if months with RSAFS growth above 0.7% do not produce above-baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the signal decays entirely after accounting for noise.

mechanism_narrative: A broad-based demand impulse requires simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS; when both are rising, forward earnings expectations strengthen across cyclicals and the S&P 500 tends to post positive returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 recent quarterly growth > 0 and RSAFS 3-month growth > 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 13277.4 in the 2010-07 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-retail expansion does not outperform either series individually, or if 60d forward SPX returns are not positive in most triggered cases.
